what_it_does: Implements a variable, data-driven polar projection engine that maps timestamped events into a frame (centre, angle, radius, recursive depth) according to a swappable binding. It resolves angular sectors from live registries or from distinct values in the data, computes radii from time, tree distance, semantic cosine distance, two-pole separation, or nucleation typing, and surfaces human-readable labels and summaries for operators.
observations: All sector divisions are resolved from the binding's angle_from source (kind, kind-group, registry rows, graph nodes) rather than hardcoded.
Multiple legibility registries (kinds, node-types, families, projection spaces, cascade flow) are imported defensively with empty-dict fallbacks.
Vector-similarity primitives (_cosine, _spearman, _centroid, _normv) are replicated locally to keep the projection floor dependency-free.
A fail-loud separation_report tests whether two pole vectors actually separate the corpus before a normalized radius is trusted.
A nucleation_report types items against registered types, identifies candidate new types and dissolution candidates, and returns per-item placement.
BindingRegistry discovers lens definitions from bindings/*.py and raises ValueError for malformed rows.
project() supports several radius modes: time, semantic, separator, nucleation, and structural tree distance.
Operator-facing summaries are filtered by kind to avoid leaking machine tokens or addresses.
standouts: The separation_report is an explicit anti-green-paint gate: it checks pole distinctness, per-pole spread, Spearman redundancy, and balance before declaring a two-gravity field valid.
The nucleation_report implements a 20/80 water-law with a pile-permutation null test for distinctness and bounded agglomerative clustering.
The engine treats the projection instrument as fully variable: no privileged default sectors, and even the fallback binding is synthesized from data.
conventions: Fail-loud validation for required inputs (separator poles, semantic centre vector, malformed binding rows).
Defensive imports with empty-dict fallback for optional registries.
Pure-read functions receive all external data (events, vectors, poles, sector ids/edges) as arguments rather than fetching from stores.
Operator-law: machine ids and addresses are hidden; human-readable names and guarded summaries are produced.
Nested helper functions for local scope (_guard, segs, ranks, _std, _pick, _margin, _is_c).
Deterministic ordering via stable tie-breakers and a fixed random seed for nucleation nulls.
concerns: The file is long and mixes many responsibilities: binding registry, vector math, sector resolution, multiple radius modes, humanization, separation, nucleation, and projection orchestration.
Several radius modes add precompute blocks inside project(), increasing its cyclomatic complexity and making isolated testing harder.
The nucleation path imports runtime.scale lazily inside the function, which can raise at runtime if scale is unavailable.
The truncated source means the end of project() and the _is_c helper are not fully visible, leaving some behavior unverifiable from the excerpt.
notes: This is the core projection floor: a pure-read, binding-driven coordinate generator that refuses to hardcode sectors or radii and explicitly tests whether a two-pole field actually separates before trusting it.
questions: What is the exact role and implementation of the nested project._is_c helper?
How does the nucleation radius mode integrate with the rest of project() given the file truncation?
Are there unit tests covering the separation_report degeneracy cases and the nucleation permutation null?
What is the expected shape and source of the vectors and poles dictionaries passed by callers?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a universal, variable projection engine with no hardcoded sectors; the file matches that purpose and additionally provides separation/nucleation analytics and operator-facing humanization.
intention_signals: The file is trying to build a projection instrument that adapts its coordinate system to whatever data and bindings are present, while surfacing only human-meaningful labels and rigorously checking whether derived fields (semantic, separator, nucleation) have real structure rather than normalized noise.
novelty: It is distinctive that the engine refuses fixed axes and includes explicit separation and nucleation honesty gates rather than relying solely on normalized radii.
contribution: It contributes a pure-read, binding-driven coordinate generator plus honesty gates (separation_report, nucleation_report) that prevent normalized visualizations from masking noise, and a humanization layer that keeps operator-facing output free of machine tokens.
summary_for_embedding: A variable polar projection engine that resolves sectors and radii from live bindings and data, with vector-similarity primitives, two-gravity separation validation, 20/80 type nucleation, and operator-facing humanization.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a universal, data-driven projection floor that refuses fixed axes and validates whether derived geometric fields actually carry signal before rendering them.

symbols.does: Converts a machine sector or kind id into a human-readable Title-Case label.
symbols.description: Splits the id on dots, underscores, dashes, and slashes, then title-cases the resulting words. Used as the fallback when no declared registry name exists.

symbols.does: Returns the declared human name for a kind or sector, falling back to a humanized id.
symbols.description: Looks up the kind in the supplied meta registry and returns meta.name if present; otherwise returns _humanize_kind(kind). Selects among kind, node-type, group, projection-space, or cascade-flow registries.

symbols.does: Returns the declared one-line meaning for a kind or sector, if any.
symbols.description: Looks up the kind in the supplied meta registry and returns meta.is when available; returns None for unseeded kinds so no meaning is fabricated.

symbols.does: Extracts operator-facing text from an event summary, stripping machine prefixes and addresses.
symbols.description: For a small allow-listed set of human-content kinds (chat, annotation, trial.turn) it strips the known machine prefix and returns the human tail; for all other kinds it returns empty because the meaning lives in kind_meaning.

symbols.does: Sanitizes a kept human summary tail by removing machine addresses and tidying separators.
symbols.description: Removes any token matching a scheme://address pattern, collapses whitespace, and trims dangling punctuation; returns empty if the tail becomes only machine residue.

symbols.does: Discovers and holds lens bindings from the bindings directory.
symbols.description: Provides file-based discovery of BINDING dicts in bindings/*.py, fail-loud validation, and retrieval of a default or requested binding.

symbols.does: Initializes an empty binding row store.
symbols.description: Creates the internal _rows dictionary used to hold discovered bindings keyed by id.

symbols.does: Scans bindings directories and loads valid BINDING dicts.
symbols.description: Defaults to an absolute repo-root bindings path so discovery works regardless of cwd; imports each .py file, validates required BINDING_FIELDS and id==stem, and raises ValueError for malformed rows.

symbols.does: Returns a binding by id, or a sensible default binding.
symbols.description: Returns the requested binding if present; otherwise falls back to raw, the first declared binding, or a synthesized data-driven raw-kinds binding so the instrument always has a lens.

symbols.does: Lists all discovered bindings sorted by id.
symbols.description: Returns a sorted list of binding dicts for enumeration or UI presentation.

symbols.does: Maps a string to a deterministic float in [0,1].
symbols.description: Hashes the string with SHA-256 and converts the first eight hex digits to a normalized float; used for stable deterministic placement.

symbols.does: Parses an ISO timestamp string into a datetime, or None.
symbols.description: Wraps datetime.fromisoformat with exception handling; used to filter events to those with valid timestamps.

symbols.does: Parses a scrubber value into an aware datetime.
symbols.description: Accepts epoch seconds or ISO string and returns a timezone-aware datetime; returns None for missing or invalid values so the caller uses real now.

symbols.does: Extracts the primary address from an event.
symbols.description: Returns the event's address or source_address, or an empty string if neither is present.

symbols.does: Computes tree distance between two scheme-agnostic addresses.
symbols.description: Strips any scheme prefix, splits on /, and returns (len(a)-common)+(len(b)-common) where common is the common prefix length; 0 for exact match, 1 for parent/child, 2 for siblings, etc.

symbols.does: Normalizes an address into path segments for tree distance.
symbols.description: Removes the ui:// scheme prefix if present and returns non-empty slash-separated segments.

symbols.does: Computes cosine similarity between two vectors.
symbols.description: Fail-loud on dimension mismatch or zero vectors; replicated locally so the projection floor has no dependency on nodes/retrieve.

symbols.does: Computes Spearman rank correlation with average-rank ties.
symbols.description: Ranks both lists, handles ties with average ranks, then computes Pearson on ranks; returns 0.0 for fewer than two points or zero variance.

symbols.does: Assigns 1-based average ranks to a list of values.
symbols.description: Sorts values, groups equal values, and assigns each group the mean of its rank range; used by _spearman.

symbols.does: Tests whether two pole vectors genuinely separate a corpus.
symbols.description: Computes raw pole distinctness, per-pole cosine spread, Spearman redundancy, and balance; returns a report including separates boolean and leader examples.

symbols.does: Computes population standard deviation of raw cosines.
symbols.description: Used to measure how much each pole's pull varies across the corpus; returns 0.0 for fewer than two values.

symbols.does: Builds leader detail records for selected item indices.
symbols.description: Maps indices to refs, signed lean, and per-pole pulls for the separation report's leader lists.

symbols.does: Unit-normalizes a vector.
symbols.description: Divides each component by the Euclidean magnitude (or 1.0 if zero); used for centroid and direction calculations.

symbols.does: Computes the unit-normalized mean of a set of vectors.
symbols.description: Sums component-wise and normalizes the result; represents a group's centre direction.

symbols.does: Types items against registered types and reports candidate new types and dissolution candidates.
symbols.description: Uses each type's own admission radius for membership, agglomerates bounded worst-fitting pile items, tests distinctness against a permutation null, applies a 20/80 birth threshold, and returns per-item placement plus candidate and dissolution metadata.

symbols.does: Computes silhouette-style distinctness for a local cluster.
symbols.description: Returns mean internal cosine to the cluster centroid minus mean external cosine to the nearest existing type; used to judge whether a pile is a real new type.

symbols.does: Converts a plural registry name to the singular event field name.
symbols.description: Applies a simple rule (ies->y, trailing s dropped) so events can be mapped to registry rows via a single convention.

symbols.does: Maps an event to the row id of the entity-set named by angle_from.
symbols.description: For graph modes returns node or from_node; for registry modes returns the singular field; returns None when the event names no row, producing an honest remainder sector.

symbols.does: Deterministically topologically sorts ids using directed edges.
symbols.description: Kahn's algorithm with a stable key tie-breaker; cycles are appended stably rather than dropped.

symbols.does: Resolves ordered sector ids from a binding's angle_from source.
symbols.description: Handles kind, kind-group, registry rows, and graph nodes; supports count or edge ordering; adds a remainder sector for unmapped events when honest.

symbols.does: Finds the wheel index for a kind or row given the sector map.
symbols.description: Supports grouped bindings via glob matching and falls back to the last sector for unmapped values.

symbols.does: Maps an address to a deterministic dyadic grid cell.
symbols.description: Strips scheme, hashes each path segment to one of four quadrants, and builds a (i,j,d) coordinate MSB-first so parent cells contain child cells.

symbols.does: Projects timestamped events into polar coordinates according to a binding.
symbols.description: Filters and timestamps events, resolves sectors, then computes per-point angle and radius based on the binding's radius_from mode (time, semantic, separator, nucleation, or tree distance). Surfaces normalized and raw values plus separation and nucleation reports.

symbols.does: Nested helper inside project; exact logic truncated in the excerpt.
symbols.description: Appears to classify or filter an item index during projection; its full behavior is not visible in the provided source.
